name: Chocolate Phyllo
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw the phyllo pack and remove 3 sheets, refolding and returning the remainder to the freezer.
2. Cover the 3 sheets with a slightly damp kitchen towel.
3. Melt the clarified butter in the microwave and whisk it into the cocoa powder until smooth.
4. Place half a sheet of parchment on a baking sheet and reserve.
5. Lay one sheet of phyllo on a stainless or marble surface.
6. Using a pastry brush, thoroughly baste the sheet with the cocoa mixture, stroking from the center outwards to avoid tearing.
7. Sprinkle sugar over medium portions.
8. Add another sheet of phyllo and repeat steps 6-7 through three sheets.
9. Lift the stack with a pastry spatula and place it upside-down on the baking parchment in the baking sheet.
10. Baste the bottom (now upside) of the stack with chocolate, coating it evenly.
11. Sprinkle sugar over the top.
12. Cover with another half-sized sheet of parchment.
13. Place a second baking sheet on top and weigh down with an 8-inch cast iron skillet.
14. Bake at 350°F for 15 minutes until the phyllo is crisp.
15. Remove the cast iron skillet, upper baking sheet, and parchment, allowing it to cool on a rack for 10 minutes.
16. Remove scraps and break into pieces to feed the children; use the shapes to garnish your favorite chocolate dessert.
